marketState: bearish — storage surplus, negative seasonality, and slight fair-value overvaluation; extreme near-term heat provides temporary support only
summary: Front month (Aug 2026) at $3.279 trades $0.32 above the model fair value of $2.96, sitting above the midpoint of the $2.46–$3.46 fair-value band and modestly overvalued in every forward scenario through 21 days. Near-term weather is extreme — 1-7D demand is above the 5-year maximum (+44% vs 5y, aboveMax=true) — but this collapses sharply to just +11% vs 5y in the 8-16D window (aboveMax=false), meaning the heat premium is already fading (-7.5 BCF/d in 24h) and should unwind within one to two weeks. Seasonal probabilities are decisively negative: only a 19% chance price is higher in 4 weeks with a typical 4-week drift of -1.8%, and storage sits +151 Bcf above the 5-year average after a 6 Bcf bearish injection surprise. The sole structural offset is managed-money positioning at the 18th percentile 5y (heavily net-short, ratio 0.71), which creates short-squeeze risk and argues against aggressive short sizing; September seasonality (+3.1%, historically the strongest month) also represents a medium-term counter-rally risk before the thesis fully plays out.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonal drift averages -1.8% over the next 4 weeks with only a 19% forward probability of a higher price — the weakest reading in the dataset — and the typical-year path implies further underperformance as price is already running +3.3pp above the median YTD trajectory. Conviction is medium rather than high because managed money is at only the 18th percentile net short — a crowded, squeeze-prone position — and September's historical +3.1% average creates a meaningful counter-rally window before TP2 is reached.

shortTerm.rationale: Short-term signals are genuinely conflicted and do not support an immediate entry. The 1-7D demand is above the 5-year maximum — a hard near-term bullish catalyst that could push prices toward $3.40 in the next 1-2 days — while the 4-hour chart is the only clearly bullish timeframe (RSI 50.3, positive MACD histogram, 50>200 EMA). Simultaneously, 5m and 15m RSIs are near-oversold (34–34.5) with negative MACD, and the 8-16D demand has already dropped from above-max to just 11% vs 5y, confirming the heat is fading. Timing the exact demand-fade turn within days carries too much noise given the mixed timeframe signals and low seasonality reliability (51pp 10-90 spread). The cleaner risk-adjusted approach is to wait for the heat-driven bounce to provide a better short entry near $3.38–3.45, or for a confirmed breakdown below $3.20 as a signal of accelerating demand retreat.
shortTerm.trigger: Enter SHORT at 3.38–3.45 if price rallies on residual heat-spike (stop 3.52, TP 3.10–2.95); OR enter SHORT on daily close below 3.20 as confirmed signal of accelerating demand retreat and fading heat premium
